questions.body: An arrow shot vertically upwards loses its initial speed by 60% in 3 seconds. The maximum height reached by the arrow is (g = 9.8 ms^-2)
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Let initial speed be v. So, after 3 second speed will be = (2/5)v. From first equation of motion, v = u - gt, (2/5)v = v - g×3 ⇒ v = 5g ....(i). From third equation of motion, v² = u² - 2ga. Since at maximum height, v = 0. So, 0 = v² - 2gH, H = v²/2g. From equation (i) H = (5g)²/2g = 122.5m

questions.body: Bromine can be liberated from KBr solution by the action of
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution: (B) KBr(aq) + Cl₂(aq) → KCl(aq) + Br₂(aq). More reactive non-metal displaces less reactive non-metal from its salt solution.

questions.body: A ball is dropped from a height of 7.2 m. It bounces back to 3.2 m after striking the floor. The ball remains in contact with the floor for 20 ms. Given that g = 10 ms^-2, the average acceleration of the ball during the contact is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: If mass is m of body As at the impact k₁ = 72 m = 1/2 mu² ⇒ u = − 12 m/s (down ward) k₁ = 32 m = 1/2 mv² ⇒ v = 8 m/s (up ward) ⇒ ΔP = m (v−u) ⇒ ΔP = m [8 − (− 12)] = 20 m Impulse, F• t = ΔP ⇒ m × aₐᵥₑ × 20 × 10⁻³ = 20m ⇒ aₐᵥₑ = 1000 m/s²

questions.body: In ΔABC, D is a point on side AC such that ∠ABD = ½ ∠ABC. If AB = 36, BC = 48, CD = 28, then the length DA will be
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: In ΔABC, BD works as a angle bisector so by angle bisector theorem AB/BC = AD/DC 36/48 = x/28 x = 3/4 × 28 = 21

questions.body: A loaded bus (mass m₁) and an unloaded bus (mass m₂) are both moving with the same kinetic energy. Brakes are applied to both the buses so as to exert equal retarding force. If S₁ and S₂ are the distances covered by the two buses respectively, before coming to rest, then S₁/S₂ is.
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: m₂ > m₁ K₂ = K₁ If both comes to rest then, ⇒ ΔK₂ = ΔK₁ As F₂ = F₁ = F So, W₂ = W₁ ⇒ F × s₁ = F × s₂ ⇒ s₁ = s₂ ⇒ s₁/s₂ = 1

questions.body: In a neon discharge tube 2.8 × 10¹⁸ Ne⁺ ions move to the right per second while 1.2 × 10¹⁸ electrons move to the left per second. Therefore, the current in the discharge tube is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: As current is considered as the flow of positive charge so, net flow of charge towards right or net flow of positive charge = ne Δs I = ne/t, here t = 1 s So, I = (1.2 + 2.8) × 10¹⁸ × 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹ A = 0.64 A (towards right)

questions.body: Three equal resistances are connected as shown in the figure. Maximum power that can be dissipated by each resistance is 40 watt. Therefore, the maximum power that can be safely dissipated in the combination is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: As I²R = 40 W in the given combination, Req = 3R/2 ⇒ Pmax which can be safely derived = I² × (3R/2) = 3/2 × I²R = 3/2 × 40 = 60 W

questions.body: If (b + c - a)/a = (c + a - b)/b and (a + b - c)/c are in A.P. and a + b + c ≠ 0, then
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: (b+c-a)/a, (c+a-b)/b and (a+b-c)/c are in A.P. ∴ (b+c-a)/a + 2, (c+a-b)/b + 2 and (a+b-c)/c + 2 are also in AP ∴ (a+b+c)/a, (a+b+c)/b and (a+b+c)/c are in AP ∴ 1/a + 1/b and 1/c are in AP ∴ 1/a = 1/c, 1/b = 1/a = 1/c ⇒ 2/b = (a+c)/(ac) ⇒ b = 2ac/(a+c)

questions.body: How much water should be filled ina container 21 cm in height, so that it appears half filled when viewed from the top of the container? [Refractive index of water = 4/3]
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Here apparent depth = 21 - x real depth = x ⇒ μ = R.D./A.D. = x/(21-1) ⇒ 4/3 = x/(21-x) ⇒ 4 × 21 - 4x = 3x 4 × 21' = 7x x = 21

Q = xs₁t₁, Q = y × (1 cal)/(g°C) t₂

questions.body: An aeroplane is flying horizontally at a height of 3150 m above a horizontal plane ground. At a particular instant it passes another aeroplane vertically below it. At this instant, the angles of elevation of the planes from a point on the ground are 30° and 60°. Hence, the distance between the planes at that instant is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
In ΔADC
tan 60° = AC/DC
√3 = 3150/DC
DC = 3150/√3
ΔDBC
tan 30° = BC/DC
1/√3 = BC/(3150/√3)
BC = (3150/√3) × (1/√3) = 3150/3 = 1050 m
distance between the plane
= AB = AC - BC
= 3150 - 1050
= 2100

questions.body: A boy throws a stone (mass 100g) vertically upwards. It reaches a height of 10 m and then falls to the ground. The work done by the boy is (g = 10 m/s^2)
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
Work is done by the body against the force of gravitation when it moves upwards, W = mgh
W = Fs cosθ = mgh cosθ°
= 100g × 1.0 m/s² × 10
= 0.1 × 100 = 10J

questions.subject: chemistry

questions.body: A conical vessel of radius 6 cm and height 8 cm is completely filled with water. A metal sphere is now lowered into the water. The size of the sphere is such that when it touches the inner surface, it just gets immersed. The fraction of water that overflows from the conical vessel is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
h = 8

ΔABE ~ ΔADC
AB/AD = BE/DC = AE/AC
AB/8 = R/6 = AE/10
AB/8 = R/6 = AE/10
AE = 10R/6 = 5R/3
AD = AE + DE
8 = 5R/3 + R
8 = 8R/3
R = 3
Function of water flow
= volume of sphere/volume of cone
= (4/3)πR³/((1/3)πr²h) = (4(3)³)/(6² × 8) = 3/8

A = sqrt(A² + A² + 2A² cos(2θ))
A = sqrt(2A² + 2A² + cos(2θ))
if and only if, cos 2θ = – 1/2
the 2θ = 120°
θ = 60°

questions.body: Which of the following hydroxides on heating decomposes to liberate oxygen as one of the products?
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
(a + b - b - c) + c (b + c - c - a)
= 1/2 [a(c - b) + b(a - c) + c (b - a)]
= 1/2 [ac - ab + ba - bc + cb - ca]
= 1/2 [0]
= 0

(A) Copper hydroxide and zinc hydroxide on heating decomposes to liberate water. Sodium hydroxide melts on heating. Mercury hydroxide decomposes on heating to liberate oxygen
